title: Espeletia laxiflora
intro: Espeletia laxiflora, also known as 'Roldana', is a flowering plant species of the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to South America, particularly in the Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Espeletia laxiflora include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in tinctures and capsules, to leverage its health benefits and provide relief from various ailments.
taxonomy: Espeletia laxiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Espeletia, which contains several species of plants native to the Andes mountains. Espeletia laxiflora is further classified as a member of the tribe Espeletieae, within the subfamily Carduoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. Espeletia laxiflora is a unique and endemic species found in the high-altitude regions of South America.
